問題文: A bicycle, as a light vehicle, may travel on the road shoulder located on the left side of the roadway, as long as it does not significantly obstruct pedestrian traffic.
解説: Under the Road Traffic Act, light vehicles may travel on the road shoulder on the left side of the roadway at a speed and in a manner that does not obstruct pedestrians.
法令参照: Articles 17 and 17-2 of the Road Traffic Act: Regulations on how light vehicles use road shoulders and their relationship with pedestrians.

問題文: An ordinary bicycle must use the bicycle lane on roads where such a lane is provided, except in unavoidable circumstances.
解説: Under Article 63-3, when a bicycle lane exists, an ordinary bicycle is obligated to use that bicycle lane in principle.
法令参照: Article 63-3 of the Road Traffic Act: Obligation for ordinary bicycles to use bicycle lanes when provided.

問題文: Light vehicles, including bicycles, must not ride side by side with other light vehicles in principle.
解説: Article 19 of the Road Traffic Act prohibits light vehicles from riding side by side, with certain exceptions for ordinary bicycles.
法令参照: Article 19 of the Road Traffic Act: Basic rule prohibiting side-by-side riding of light vehicles.

問題文: When a bicycle is near a location where a bicycle crossing lane is provided, it must cross the road using that bicycle crossing lane.
解説: Under Article 63-6, when crossing a road near a bicycle crossing lane, bicycles must cross using that lane.
法令参照: Article 63-6 of the Road Traffic Act: Method of crossing where a bicycle crossing lane is provided.

問題文: Even at intersections with a stop line and a “Stop” sign, a bicycle does not need to stop if no vehicles are around.
解説: Under Article 43, at designated locations, a complete stop is required before the stop line regardless of the presence of other vehicles.
法令参照: Article 43 of the Road Traffic Act: Obligation to stop at locations with a stop sign.

問題文: Vehicles including bicycles must stop immediately before the stop line when passing a railroad crossing and ensure safety.
解説: Under Article 33, bicycles must stop and ensure safety when passing a railroad crossing.
法令参照: Article 33 of the Road Traffic Act: Obligation to stop and ensure safety when passing a railroad crossing.

問題文: It is prohibited to operate a bicycle equipped with braking devices that do not meet standards and may cause traffic danger.
解説: Under Article 63-9, bicycles with braking devices that do not meet standards and may cause danger must not be operated.
法令参照: Article 63-9 of the Road Traffic Act: Standards for bicycle braking devices and prohibition on operating unsafe bicycles.

問題文: A bicycle operator must not ride at night without a compliant reflector or tail lamp.
解説: Under Article 63-9, bicycles must be equipped with a compliant reflector or tail lamp when traveling at night.
法令参照: Article 63-9 of the Road Traffic Act: Obligation to equip bicycles with reflectors or tail lamps at night.

問題文: A bicycle must use its headlamp when traveling at night.
解説: Under Article 52, bicycles must use a headlamp when traveling at night.
法令参照: Article 52 of the Road Traffic Act: Obligation to use a headlamp at night.

問題文: A bicycle operator may ride on the sidewalk while continuously ringing the bell to make pedestrians move aside.
解説: Under Article 54, audible warning devices may only be used when required by law or to prevent danger, and continuous ringing to force pedestrians aside is not permitted.
法令参照: Article 54 of the Road Traffic Act: When audible warning devices must and must not be used.

問題文: Regardless of surrounding conditions, as long as the speed limit is obeyed, any riding method is acceptable for bicycles.
解説: Article 70 requires operating at a speed and in a manner that does not endanger others, according to road and traffic conditions.
法令参照: Article 70 of the Road Traffic Act: Regulations concerning the obligation of safe driving.

問題文: Operating a bicycle while staring at a smartphone screen when the bicycle is not stopped may constitute a violation of the safe driving obligation.
解説: Looking at a mobile phone screen while riding is considered inattention to the front and violates the safe‑driving obligation.
法令参照: Articles 70 and 71 of the Road Traffic Act: Regulations on safe driving and the use of mobile phones.

問題文: Riding a bicycle while unable to properly operate the handlebars, such as when holding an umbrella or using one hand, may constitute a safe driving obligation violation.
解説: Riding in a manner that prevents proper operation of the handlebars or brakes may violate the safe‑driving obligation under Article 70.
法令参照: Articles 70 and 71 of the Road Traffic Act: Regulations on riding in a manner that interferes with proper handle operation.

問題文: Adult bicycle users are legally encouraged to wear a bicycle helmet.
解説: Under Article 63-11, bicycle riders of all ages are encouraged to wear a helmet.
法令参照: Article 63-11 of the Road Traffic Act: Helmet-wearing encouragement duty for bicycle riders.

問題文: A person responsible for a child must ensure that the child wears a bicycle helmet when riding a bicycle.
解説: Article 63-11 also imposes a duty on guardians to encourage children to wear helmets.
法令参照: Article 63-11 of the Road Traffic Act: Helmet-wearing encouragement duty for children and infants.

問題文: A bicycle must not travel on the right side of the roadway even on a one-lane road.
解説: Light vehicles must travel on the left side of the roadway, and right‑side riding is generally prohibited due to increased collision risk.
法令参照: Articles 17 and 18 of the Road Traffic Act: Regulations on traffic lane usage and the principle of left-side travel.

問題文: Wearing reflective materials or bright-colored clothing at night is considered desirable from the standpoint of legal duty of care and safe driving.
解説: Increasing visibility at night, such as using reflective materials, is strongly recommended for accident prevention.
法令参照: Article 70 of the Road Traffic Act: Concept of ensuring visibility at night as part of safe driving obligations.

問題文: On school routes and near schools, bicycles are required to pay extra attention to speed and riding position for the safety of children.
解説: In areas with high risk of children suddenly entering the roadway, extra caution is required under the safe‑driving obligation.
法令参照: Article 76 and related school-route designations: Duty of care to ensure safety on school routes.

問題文: On steep downhill slopes, as long as the speed is below the speed limit, riding at any speed does not constitute a violation of the safe driving obligation.
解説: Depending on slope and road conditions, riding may be dangerous even below the speed limit, requiring appropriate speed adjustment.
法令参照: Article 70 of the Road Traffic Act: Obligation to maintain safe speed on downhill slopes.

問題文: Even in rainy weather or strong winds, there is no need to change bicycle riding methods as long as the speed limit is observed.
解説: In bad weather, braking distance increases and visibility decreases, requiring slower and more cautious riding.
法令参照: Article 70 of the Road Traffic Act: Speed adjustment obligations during rain or strong winds.

問題文: A bicycle, as a light vehicle, may travel on the road shoulder located on the left side of the roadway, as long as it does not significantly obstruct pedestrian traffic.
解説: Under Article 17-2, light vehicles may travel on the left‑side road shoulder at a speed and in a manner that does not obstruct pedestrians.
法令参照: Article 17-2 of the Road Traffic Act: Regulations on light vehicles using road shoulders and consideration for pedestrians.

問題文: Under the Road Traffic Act Enforcement Regulations, an ordinary bicycle must have four wheels or fewer.
解説: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-2-2 requires ordinary bicycles to have four wheels or fewer.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-2-2: Standards for the number of wheels on ordinary bicycles.

問題文: The standards for an ordinary bicycle include the requirement that it must not have a sidecar attached.
解説: Article 9-2-2(2)(a) specifies that ordinary bicycles must not have a sidecar.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-2-2: Whether ordinary bicycles may have sidecars.

問題文: An ordinary bicycle may be equipped with a child seat as a passenger seat other than the operator’s seat.
解説: The Enforcement Regulation allows only child seats as passenger seats other than the operator’s seat.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-2-2: Restrictions on seats installed on ordinary bicycles.

問題文: The braking devices of an ordinary bicycle must be installed in a position that can be easily operated while riding.
解説: Article 9-2-2(2)(d) requires braking devices to be located where they can be easily operated while riding.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-2-2: Standards for the location of braking device controls.

問題文: An ordinary bicycle may have sharp protrusions that could endanger pedestrians.
解説: The same article prohibits sharp protrusions that may endanger pedestrians.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-2-2: Prohibition of sharp protrusions that may endanger pedestrians.

問題文: Under the braking standards for bicycles, it is sufficient if either the front or rear wheel can be braked.
解説: Article 9-3(1) requires braking devices capable of braking both the front and rear wheels.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-3: Standards for bicycle braking devices (front and rear braking).

問題文: A bicycle’s braking device must be capable of smoothly stopping within 3 meters when traveling at an initial speed of 10 km/h on a dry, flat, paved surface.
解説: Article 9-3(2) specifies the detailed braking performance requirements.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-3: Bicycle braking performance (stopping within 3 m from 10 km/h).

問題文: Reflective materials installed on a bicycle must be easily visible when illuminated by headlights from 100 meters behind at night.
解説: Article 9-4(1) requires reflectors to be visible when illuminated from 100 meters behind at night.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-4: Standards for visibility of bicycle reflectors (visible from 100 m at night).

問題文: The color of the reflected light from bicycle reflectors may be yellow or green.
解説: The Enforcement Regulation limits reflector colors to orange or red; yellow or green do not meet standards.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-4: Standards for reflector color.

問題文: The motor used to assist human power on a bicycle (electric-assist bicycle) must be an electric motor.
解説: Article 1-3(1)(a) requires the motor to be an electric motor.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-3: Type of motor used for bicycles that assist human power (electric motor).

問題文: An electric-assist bicycle must be constructed so that motor assistance does not operate at speeds of 24 km/h or higher.
解説: Article 1-3(1)(c) requires that motor assistance does not operate at speeds of 24 km/h or higher.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-3: Motor assistance must not operate at speeds of 24 km/h or higher.

問題文: A light vehicle using a motor must use an electric motor and be constructed to be operated while walking.
解説: Article 1-2-3(2)(a)(b) requires the use of an electric motor and operation while walking.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-2-3: Structure of motorized light vehicles (operated while walking, etc.).

問題文: A mobility scooter must be constructed so that it cannot exceed a speed of 6 km/h.
解説: Article 1-4(2)(b) requires that the vehicle cannot exceed 6 km/h.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-4: Maximum speed limit for mobility scooters.

問題文: The motor of a mobility scooter may be an internal combustion engine such as a gasoline engine.
解説: Article 1-4(2)(a) requires the use of an electric motor, not an internal combustion engine.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-4: Type of motor for mobility scooters (electric motor).

問題文: An electric-assist bicycle that meets the standards of Article 1-3 of the Enforcement Regulations is treated as a “bicycle,” not a motorized bicycle.
解説: Vehicles meeting these standards are treated as bicycles, as indicated by the National Police Agency and prefectural authorities.
法令参照: Articles 2 and Enforcement Regulation 1-3: Definitions distinguishing electric-assist bicycles from motorized bicycles.

問題文: A bicycle equipped with a braking device on only one wheel may violate the braking performance requirements of Road Traffic Act Article 63-9.
解説: The same article requires bicycles to be equipped with braking devices, meaning effective brakes on both wheels.
法令参照: Article 63-9(1) of the Road Traffic Act: Legal basis for braking device standards for bicycles.

問題文: A bicycle’s reflector must have performance that allows its reflected light to be easily confirmed from the illumination point when lit from 100 meters behind at night.
解説: This requirement is explicitly stated in Article 9-4(1) of the Enforcement Regulation.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-4: Reflectors must be visible when illuminated from 100 m behind at night.

問題文: Motorized bicycles and automobiles are not permitted to enter or travel on bicycle lanes designated by road signs.
解説: Bicycle lanes are exclusive lanes for bicycles as light vehicles, and other vehicles are prohibited from entering.
法令参照: Articles 17 and 63-3 of the Road Traffic Act: Bicycle lanes are for bicycles; motorized bicycles and cars may not use them.

問題文: Under Road Traffic Act Article 2(3), a person walking while pushing a bicycle is regarded as a pedestrian.
解説: This article provides that a person pushing a vehicle is regarded as a pedestrian, and this applies to bicycles as well.
法令参照: Article 2(3) of the Road Traffic Act: A person pushing a vehicle is treated as a pedestrian.

問題文: When a bicycle crossing lane is provided near the crossing point, a bicycle must cross the road using that bicycle crossing lane.
解説: This is the basic rule for how bicycles must cross the road under Articles 25-2 and 63-6 of the Road Traffic Act.
法令参照: Articles 25-2 and 63-6 of the Road Traffic Act: Bicycles must cross using the bicycle crossing lane where provided.

問題文: Riding bicycles side by side is prohibited under Road Traffic Act Articles 19 and 63-5, except in sections specifically permitted by road signs.
解説: Riding side by side on light vehicles including bicycles is prohibited in principle, with exceptions only where signs permit it.
法令参照: Articles 19 and 63-5 of the Road Traffic Act: Side-by-side riding of bicycles is prohibited except where signs allow it.

問題文: The number of children who may ride on a bicycle and the structure of child seats are restricted by Road Traffic Act Article 63-4 and prefectural regulations, and unlimited boarding is not allowed.
解説: The number and placement of child seats and the number of children allowed to ride are limited by laws and regulations.
法令参照: Article 63-4 of the Road Traffic Act: Regulations on the number of passengers and child seats on bicycles.

問題文: When traveling on roads at night, a bicycle must use a headlamp and be equipped with a tail lamp or a reflector that meets the standards of Enforcement Regulation Article 9-4.
解説: This is the obligation regarding lighting and reflectors under Articles 52 and 63-9(2) of the Road Traffic Act.
法令参照: Articles 52 and 63-9(2) of the Road Traffic Act: Nighttime lighting and reflector obligations.

問題文: Under the Road Traffic Act, a bicycle is defined as a light vehicle.
解説: A bicycle is classified as a light vehicle under Article 2(1)(11) of the Road Traffic Act and is subject to various obligations as a vehicle.
法令参照: Article 2(1)(11) of the Road Traffic Act: Bicycles are defined as light vehicles.

問題文: A bicycle must, in principle, travel along the left edge of the roadway.
解説: Under Articles 17 and 63-3, bicycles must travel along the left edge of the roadway.
法令参照: Articles 17 and 63-3 of the Road Traffic Act: Bicycles must travel along the left edge of the roadway.

問題文: Even for bicycles, riding on the right side of the roadway (so‑called “wrong‑way riding”) is a violation of the Road Traffic Act.
解説: As a light vehicle, a bicycle must travel on the left side of the road, and right‑side riding is generally prohibited.
法令参照: Article 17 of the Road Traffic Act: Right-side riding (wrong-way riding) is prohibited for bicycles.

問題文: When a bicycle travels on a sidewalk, it must give priority to pedestrians, travel slowly, and must not obstruct pedestrian traffic.
解説: Under Article 63-4, bicycles must give priority to pedestrians and travel slowly when using sidewalks.
法令参照: Article 63-4 of the Road Traffic Act: Bicycles must give priority to pedestrians and travel slowly on sidewalks.

問題文: As a light vehicle, a bicycle must obey traffic signals, and proceeding on a red signal constitutes a violation.
解説: Article 7 applies to all vehicles, and bicycles are no exception.
法令参照: Articles 7 and 2(1)(11) of the Road Traffic Act: Light vehicles including bicycles must obey traffic signals.

問題文: At intersections with a stop sign, bicycles are also required to make a complete stop.
解説: The obligation to stop under Article 43 also applies to bicycles as light vehicles.
法令参照: Articles 43 and 63-3 of the Road Traffic Act: The obligation to stop at intersections with a stop sign also applies to bicycles.

問題文: Riding a bicycle while holding an umbrella or with one hand may be treated as a violation of the safe‑driving obligation.
解説: This is explicitly listed as an enforcement matter by police headquarters and is considered serious negligence in accidents.
法令参照: Article 70 of the Road Traffic Act: One-handed riding and riding with an umbrella are treated as violations of the safe-driving obligation and are subject to enforcement.

問題文: Riding at night without turning on the bicycle’s headlamp is a violation of lighting obligations.
解説: Riding without lights at night violates Article 52 and is subject to enforcement nationwide.
法令参照: Articles 52 and 120 of the Road Traffic Act: Riding without lights at night is a violation of lighting obligations and subject to penalties.

問題文: Riding double on a bicycle is, in principle, prohibited under the Road Traffic Act except for special bicycles designed for carrying children.
解説: For safety reasons, riding double is not permitted except where the seat and structure are designed for it.
法令参照: Articles 57 and 63-4 of the Road Traffic Act: Riding double on a bicycle is generally prohibited except for special bicycles designed for carrying children.

問題文: A bicycle lane is a part of the roadway designated for bicycles that meet standards, and motor vehicles are generally prohibited from entering.
解説: A bicycle lane is a designated part of the roadway for bicycles, and motor vehicles are prohibited from using it.
法令参照: Article 63-3 of the Road Traffic Act: Defines which vehicles may use bicycle lanes.

問題文: At intersections designated by the Public Safety Commission, bicycles as light vehicles may be required to make a two‑stage right turn.
解説: Under Article 34, light vehicles may be required to perform a two‑stage right turn as designated by the Public Safety Commission.
法令参照: Article 34 of the Road Traffic Act: Regulations on right-turn methods for light vehicles (such as two-stage right turns).

問題文: As a light vehicle, a bicycle is subject to restrictions on the size and weight of loads according to the structure of the loading device and standards set by the Public Safety Commission.
解説: Under Article 57-2, loading methods and limits for light vehicles must comply with standards set by the Public Safety Commission.
法令参照: Article 57-2 of the Road Traffic Act: Regulations on loading limits for light vehicles.

問題文: A person who repeatedly engages in dangerous bicycle riding may be ordered by the Public Safety Commission to attend a bicycle rider training course.
解説: Under Article 108-3, repeated dangerous bicycle behavior may result in an order to attend a bicycle rider training course.
法令参照: Article 108-3 of the Road Traffic Act: Regulations on orders to attend bicycle rider training courses.

問題文: Roads marked as “Pedestrians Only” may not be traveled on by bicycles while riding.
解説: Pedestrian‑only roads are generally restricted to pedestrians, and bicycles may pass only by being pushed.
法令参照: Articles 8 and 9 of the Road Traffic Act: Regulations on pedestrian-only roads.

問題文: On roads with one‑way traffic regulations, bicycles must follow the designated direction unless exceptions are indicated by signs.
解説: One‑way traffic regulations also apply to bicycles, which must follow the indicated direction.
法令参照: Articles 8 and 17 of the Road Traffic Act: One-way traffic regulations and how bicycles must follow them.

問題文: Intentionally stopping a bicycle in the middle of the road to obstruct others’ passage constitutes a prohibited act under the Road Traffic Act.
解説: Article 76 prohibits acts that obstruct traffic, and intentionally blocking traffic is a violation.
法令参照: Article 76 of the Road Traffic Act: Prohibits acts that obstruct traffic.

問題文: Intentionally stopping a bicycle in the middle of the road to obstruct others’ passage constitutes a prohibited act under the Road Traffic Act.
解説: Article 76 prohibits acts that obstruct traffic, and intentionally blocking traffic is a violation.
法令参照: Article 76 of the Road Traffic Act: Prohibits acts that obstruct traffic.

問題文: Riding a bicycle while intoxicated is prohibited under the Road Traffic Act.
解説: Under Article 65, operating a bicycle while intoxicated is prohibited.
法令参照: Road Traffic Act Article 76: Regulations concerning prohibited acts on roads, including acts that obstruct traffic.

問題文: Riding a bicycle while listening to loud music that prevents hearing surrounding sounds may violate safe‑driving obligations and prefectural regulations.
解説: Being unable to hear surrounding sounds significantly impairs hazard perception and may be treated as a safe‑driving violation.
法令参照: Articles 70 and 71 of the Road Traffic Act: Regulations on safe driving and prohibited acts designated by the Public Safety Commission (e.g., earphones).

問題文: Because stopping distance increases with speed, adjusting speed according to conditions is required for safe driving.
解説: Stopping distance increases with speed, requiring appropriate speed selection based on visibility and road conditions.
法令参照: Article 70 of the Road Traffic Act: Obligation to select a safe speed considering stopping distance.

問題文: Even when the road surface is slippery due to ice, snow, or gravel, normal riding is acceptable as long as the speed is below the speed limit.
解説: Failing to adjust speed or path according to road conditions may violate the safe‑driving obligation.
法令参照: Article 70 of the Road Traffic Act: Obligation to adjust riding according to road surface conditions (ice, gravel, mud, etc.).

問題文: Bicycle braking performance standards specify numerical stopping distances even on wet road surfaces.
解説: The Enforcement Regulation specifies conditions for dry, flat paved surfaces but does not specify stopping distances for wet surfaces.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-3: Understanding of road surface conditions to which braking performance standards apply.

問題文: For electric‑assist bicycles, there is no upper limit on the assist ratio at low speeds (below 10 km/h).
解説: Article 1-3 sets specific upper limits for assist ratios at each speed range, including low speeds.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-3: Upper limit of assist ratio at speeds below 10 km/h.

問題文: The assist function of an electric‑assist bicycle must operate smoothly and must not interfere with safe riding.
解説: Article 1-3(2) specifies requirements for smooth and safe operation of assist functions.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-3: Standards for smoothness and safety of assist functions.

問題文: The body size of a light vehicle using a motor must not exceed 4 m in length, 2 m in width, and 3 m in height.
解説: Article 1-2-3(1) sets maximum dimensions of 4 m in length, 2 m in width, and 3 m in height.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-2-3: Maximum body size of motorized light vehicles.

問題文: In light vehicles using a motor, the motor may continue operating even when the operator has moved away from the vehicle.
解説: The same article requires the motor to stop when the operator leaves the vehicle.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-2-3: Requirement that the motor stops when the operator leaves the vehicle.

問題文: For bicycles with four or more wheels that are treated as pedestrians when pushed, the body size must not exceed 190 cm in length and 60 cm in width.
解説: Article 1-8 sets maximum dimensions of 190 cm in length and 60 cm in width for four‑wheel bicycles.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-8: Size limits for vehicles treated as pedestrians when pushed (four-wheel bicycles, etc.).

問題文: The body size of a mobility scooter must not exceed 120 cm in length, 70 cm in width, and 120 cm in height (excluding head support).
解説: Article 1-4(1) sets these dimensions as the upper limit.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-4: Size limits for mobility scooters.

問題文: The body size of a motorized vehicle for persons with disabilities must not exceed 120 cm in length, 70 cm in width, and 120 cm in height (excluding head support).
解説: Article 1-5(1) sets the same size limits as mobility scooters.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-5: Size limits for motorized vehicles for persons with disabilities.

問題文: A motorized vehicle for persons with disabilities must be clearly distinguishable in appearance from automobiles and motorized bicycles.
解説: Article 1-5(2)(b) requires that the vehicle be clearly distinguishable in appearance from automobiles and motorized bicycles.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-5: Requirement that such vehicles be distinguishable in appearance from automobiles and motorized bicycles.

問題文: Standards for bicycle helmets include ensuring field of vision, impact absorption, chin‑strap stability, and a weight of 2 kg or less.
解説: Article 9-5 sets requirements for field of vision, hearing, impact absorption, penetration resistance, chin strap, and weight of 2 kg or less; similar considerations apply to bicycle helmets.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-5: Helmet standards (field of vision, stability, weight, etc.) relevant also to bicycle helmets.

問題文: A vehicle capable of self‑propulsion by motor power alone without pedaling falls under “motor‑assisted bicycle” as defined in Enforcement Regulation Article 1‑3.
解説: Article 1-3 assumes an electric motor that assists human pedaling; vehicles that can run solely on motor power are treated as motorized bicycles.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-3: Standards for motors used in electric-assist bicycles (must be electric, must not interfere with safe riding).

問題文: For electric‑assist bicycles, the assist ratio at speeds below 10 km/h may provide motor assistance up to twice the rider’s human power.
解説: Under Article 1-3, the maximum assist ratio at speeds below 10 km/h is 1:2, and exceeding this falls outside the legal definition of a bicycle.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-3: Relationship between assist ratio and speed for electric-assist bicycles.

問題文: Electric‑assist bicycles with structures that allow easy modification of assist ratios or speed control may not meet the requirement of “not easily modifiable” under Article 1‑3.
解説: Structures that allow easy modification beyond standards do not meet the requirements for assisted bicycles and may be classified as motorized vehicles.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 1-3: Electric-assist bicycles must not be easily modifiable beyond legal standards.

問題文: Because braking performance standards are defined in Enforcement Regulation Article 9‑3, it is not sufficient for a bicycle to simply “have brakes.”
解説: This article sets specific braking performance standards, and operating a bicycle that does not meet them may violate the law.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-3: Specific braking performance standards for bicycles (e.g., stopping distance).

問題文: If a bicycle is equipped with reflectors that meet the standards of Article 9‑4, some local ordinances allow omission of a tail lamp.
解説: Many prefectural regulations allow omission of a tail lamp if a compliant reflector is installed, although using both is recommended for safety.
法令参照: Article 63-9(2) of the Road Traffic Act and Enforcement Regulation Article 9-4: Bicycles may use compliant reflectors instead of a tail lamp.

問題文: Reflectors required under Article 9‑4 must be “attached to the bicycle,” and reflective materials on clothing alone are not considered substitutes.
解説: The provision assumes the reflector is attached to the bicycle itself, and in practice attachment to the bicycle body is required.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-4: Reflectors must be attached to the bicycle itself; clothing reflectors are not considered substitutes.

問題文: Enforcement Regulation Article 5‑6 sets maximum size limits (length, width, height) for bicycles allowed to use bicycle lanes; vehicles exceeding these limits may not use them.
解説: This article sets dimensional standards for bicycles to ensure safety on bicycle lanes.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 5-6: Size standards for bicycles allowed to use bicycle lanes.

問題文: Wide four‑wheel bicycles that exceed the dimensional standards for bicycle lane use are not permitted to travel on bicycle lanes.
解説: Vehicles exceeding the dimensional limits of Article 5-6 are not permitted to use bicycle lanes.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 5-6: Only bicycles within certain size limits may use bicycle lanes.

問題文: A person pushing a bicycle across a crosswalk is regarded as a pedestrian, and automobiles must stop under Road Traffic Act Article 38.
解説: A person pushing a bicycle is treated as a pedestrian and is protected as such when crossing a crosswalk.
法令参照: Articles 38 and 2(3) of the Road Traffic Act: A person pushing a bicycle across a crosswalk is treated as a pedestrian, and vehicles must stop.

問題文: The safe use of bicycles and securing bicycle parking spaces are addressed not only by the Road Traffic Act but also by the so‑called Bicycle Act (Act on Comprehensive Bicycle Parking Measures).
解説: The Bicycle Act serves as a basic framework law promoting safe bicycle use and comprehensive parking measures.
法令参照: Bicycle Act: A law promoting safe bicycle use and comprehensive bicycle parking measures.

問題文: Enforcement Regulation Article 9‑5 prohibits operating a bicycle or other vehicle while listening to music in a way that prevents hearing surrounding sounds necessary for safe driving.
解説: This article prohibits operating a bicycle while using audio devices in a way that prevents hearing necessary sounds.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-5: Prohibits riding while listening to audio in a way that prevents hearing necessary traffic sounds.

問題文: Although reflector and tail lamp requirements mainly apply at night, conditions such as tunnels mean that it cannot be said that no equipment is needed during the daytime.
解説: Even outside nighttime, lighting may be required in tunnels, and carrying lighting equipment is recommended for safety.
法令参照: Articles 52 and 63-9(2) of the Road Traffic Act: Reflector and tail lamp obligations mainly apply at night, but daytime use is recommended for safety.

問題文: Regardless of whether a bicycle crossing lane is present, a bicycle must not cross the road if doing so may obstruct pedestrians or other normal traffic.
解説: Under Articles 25-2 and 63-7, bicycles must not cross in a manner that endangers other traffic.
法令参照: Articles 63-7 and 25-2 of the Road Traffic Act: Bicycles must not cross in a manner that obstructs other traffic.

問題文: Continuously staring at a smartphone screen while riding a bicycle may constitute a violation of the safe‑driving obligation (Road Traffic Act Article 70).
解説: This conduct is treated as failure to keep eyes forward and is subject to enforcement.
法令参照: Article 70 of the Road Traffic Act: Looking at a smartphone screen while riding may violate the safe-driving obligation.

問題文: Bicycles are required by the Enforcement Regulations to be equipped with a bell (audible warning device).
解説: Article 9-1 of the Enforcement Regulation requires bicycles to be equipped with an audible warning device similar to automobiles.
法令参照: Enforcement Regulation Article 9-1: Bicycles must be equipped with an audible warning device (bell).

問題文: Ringing a bicycle bell excessively to force pedestrians to move is not considered proper use of the audible warning device under the Road Traffic Act.
解説: Article 54 limits the use of audible warning devices to necessary situations, and misuse to force pedestrians aside is discouraged.
法令参照: Article 54 of the Road Traffic Act: Bells must be used only when necessary; misuse to force pedestrians aside is prohibited.

問題文: Currently, all bicycle users are legally encouraged to wear a bicycle helmet.
解説: The revised Road Traffic Act introduced a helmet‑wearing encouragement duty for all ages.
法令参照: Article 63-11 of the Road Traffic Act (revised): Helmet-wearing encouragement duty applies to all bicycle users.

問題文: The prohibition on riding under the influence of alcohol also applies to bicycle operation.
解説: Under Article 65, no person may operate a vehicle while intoxicated, and bicycles are included.
法令参照: Article 65 of the Road Traffic Act: Prohibits riding a bicycle while intoxicated.

問題文: Riding a bicycle without braking devices, such as so‑called “no‑brake fixies,” may result in penalties as a defective vehicle.
解説: Operating a bicycle that does not meet the requirements of Article 63-9 is subject to enforcement as a violation.
法令参照: Articles 63-9 and 120 of the Road Traffic Act: Riding a bicycle without braking devices may result in penalties.

問題文: Bicycles are obligated to travel at a safe speed appropriate to road and traffic conditions, separate from posted speed limits.
解説: Under Articles 22 and 70, bicycles must adjust speed appropriately according to conditions.
法令参照: Articles 22 and 70 of the Road Traffic Act: Bicycles must travel at a safe speed appropriate to conditions, not only within posted limits.
